gateShape.note: typecheck runs AFTER build: tsc's resolution of the @toon-protocol/swap self-reference depends on packages/swap/dist existing — running typecheck before build inflates the error count by 7 spurious 'Cannot find module' errors (false-FAIL risk). gate-correctness.ts and gate-no-regression.ts both run in this order.

method: Ran the four gate commands sequentially (lint, build, typecheck, test) against a clean `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile`, in the same environment the sandcastle implement/review runners use (node v22, pnpm 8.15.9), timing each command individually with millisecond wall-clock before/after timestamps. Reproduce: `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile`, then time `eslint .`, `pnpm -r run build`, `pnpm run typecheck`, `vitest run` in that order on a quiescent runner (no concurrent load). Includes the .sandcastle/scripts/gate-lib.test.ts suite added by this same change (swap#77), so the test counts below reflect the gate tooling's own tests too.

gateSpeed.description: CI wall-clock time for the gate the dumb zone (.sandcastle/) runs before opening a PR. Read by gate-no-regression.ts as the frozen numbers to compare live timings against.

ciWorkflowReference.note: Cross-check data point only, NOT the primary number: .github/workflows/ci.yml runs a narrower shape on a GitHub-hosted ubuntu-latest runner. The sandcastle gate above (lint/build/typecheck/test) is the shape this baseline tracks.

gatePerformance.description: Runner cost & resource efficiency, independent of wall-clock. Read by gate-no-regression.ts.

runnerMinutesBillable.note: Per the GitHub Actions run-timing API (`GET /repos/.../actions/runs/{id}/timing`) for run 30013965486, `billable.UBUNTU.total_ms` is 0 — the org runs on free/unlimited GitHub-hosted minutes, so billable runner-minutes is not a meaningful cost signal here. gate-no-regression.ts skips this check while the frozen baseline is 0. Wall-clock (gateSpeed) is the actionable metric instead.

dockerImageSize.note: Not measured in this capture pass — no Docker daemon is available in the capture sandbox. gate-no-regression.ts skips the image-size check while this is null. Reproduce in an environment with Docker: `npx --yes @ai-hero/sandcastle@0.12.0 docker build-image && docker image inspect sandcastle:swap --format '{{.Size}}'`. .github/workflows/agent-image.yml now logs the built image's size on every build so a future capture can fill this in.

cacheHitRate.sample: pnpm dependency cache (actions/setup-node@v4, cache: pnpm) across the last 5 completed CI runs on main
cacheHitRate.note: Informational only — not enforced by gate-no-regression.ts (the AC only calls out wall-clock, runner-minutes, and image size; a 5-run sample is too small to gate on without flagging false FAILs from routine cache-key rotation).

gateCorrectness.description: Trustworthiness of the gate's verdict: current violation counts, frozen here as the pre-ratchet allowlist. gate-correctness.ts fails only when live counts exceed these frozen numbers (no new false PASS); counts coming in lower than the baseline still pass (ratchet-friendly, not zero-tolerance).

typecheck.tool: tsc -p tsconfig.json --noEmit (run after build — see gateShape.note)
typecheck.note: All 25 errors are test-side (packages/swap/src/*.test.ts and packages/swap/tests/**); zero production-source (non-test src/*.ts) errors. Tracked debt: swap#69.
